title: Cloud License Manager (LM) Entitlement
text: Cloud License Manager (LM) Entitlement The Cloud License Manager provides concurrent licensing to any device connected to the internet, without the need for an RMS license manager. For a client to be able to successfully get a session from the Cloud License Manager, they will need both a Registration Token and an Authentication Token . Requirements To use a Cloud LM license, you will need: An Entitlement for Cloud LM—contact your Uniface representative to obtain an Entitlement certificate. An internet connection A Registration Token—this is maintained from within the Sentinel EMS Customer Portal. An Authentication Token (or Auth Token )—Uniface generates this when a Registration Token is used by the client for the first time. It authenticates the client when connecting to the Cloud License Manager. Note: Grace and commuter licenses are not supported with Cloud LM licensing. How Cloud LM Works Figure 1. Cloud LM licensing overview A Cloud LM Entitlement starts with an Entitlement Certificate from Uniface License Management. After activating your Entitlement on the Sentinel EMS customer portal , you can create Registration Tokens. Enter the Registration Token using the Uniface License Management utility ( ulic ) or Uniface installer. If you are changing license types, you may need to first remove LM_LICENSE_FILE from $LICENSE_OPTIONS in your assignment file. Uniface generates an Authentication Token the first time the Registration Token is used by the client. The Registration Token must be both active and have enough count for an Authentication Token to be created. Once the Authentication Token is generated for the client, the Registration Token no longer needs to be active. Uniface connects to the Cloud License Manager using the Authentication Token and allows the user to claim a session on the licensed feature, if one is available. A session is claimed when the application first uses the feature, and is returned when the application is ended. The number of sessions per feature that can be active simultaneously is determined by the terms of your Entitlement. You must have administrator access on the Sentinel EMS customer portal to be able to create Registration Tokens. Administrator access is granted by Uniface License Management. If you are not an administrator, you will need to get a Registration Token from the person administering your Sentinel licensing. Configuring and Managing Cloud LM Licenses You can use Cloud LM for your Uniface application or to deploy your Uniface Docker image. To generate a Registration Token for a Cloud LM license, see Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing . To configure a Cloud LM license, see Configuring Cloud LM Licenses . To manage Cloud LM sessions, see Managing Cloud LM Sessions and Tokens . To troubleshooting licensing errors, see Troubleshooting Cloud LM Licensing Issues . Note: Cloud LM licensing is only supported on Windows, Red Hat, CentOS, and SUSE Linux. Related Links Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing To configure your application to work with a Cloud License Manager (LM) license, you need a Registration Token. This token is used to create an Authentication Token, which allows you to claim sessions on the license. You must log in to the Sentinel EMS customer portal using an email address set as administrator by Uniface license management. Administrators can generate these tokens themselves on the Sentinel EMS customer portal, as long as they have this feature enabled for their licenses. The license owner has complete control over the Registration Tokens. Configuring Cloud LM Licenses To configure your Uniface application to connect to a Cloud LM Entitlement, you need to enter your Registration Token during installation or run the Uniface License Management utility (ulic) after installing Uniface. If you are using Cloud LM to license your Uniface Docker image, you can enter the registration token REGTOKEN environment variable when mounting your image. Managing Cloud LM Sessions and Tokens Troubleshooting Cloud LM Licensing Issues The following section provides resolution for the error which occurs while registering your Registration Token.
